title: lannea edulis var. glabrescens
intro: Lannea edulis var. glabrescens, also known as the edible Lannea, is a variant of the Lannea edulis plant species, a type of fruit-bearing shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Lannea edulis var. glabrescens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal remedy by boiling its leaves and fruits in water or using them as a tea, with some communities also using it as a poultice or applying its sap topically for skin conditions.
